Lumen narrows loss, lifts free cash flow forecast on tax savings (Reuters) -Lumen Technologies on Thursday reported a smaller-than-expected second-quarter loss and raised its full-year free cash flow forecast by more than 60%, helped by cost savings from President Trump's tax cut legislation.  Lumen now expects 2025 free cash flow between $1.2 billion and $1.4 billion, up from a prior range of $700 million to $900 million.  "We're taking it up considerably because of strong performance, but also because of the new tax bill, that definitely benefited us and that allows us to invest," CFO Chris Stansbury told Reuters in an interview.
